steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is B, $-9x^{6}+5x^{3}-8x+11$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Distribute the subtraction
steps.content:
Subtract each term inside the parentheses:
$5x^3 - 3x + 8 - (9x^6 + 5x - 3) = 5x^3 - 3x + 8 - 9x^6 - 5x + 3$

steps.title: Match to the answer choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — $-4x^{2}-8x-11$: Wrong leading degree ($x^2$ instead of $x^6$) and wrong sign on the constant. ✗
  • Choice B — $-9x^{6}+5x^{3}-8x+11$: Matches the simplified result exactly. ✓
  • Choice C — $-9x^{6}+5x^{3}+2x+5$: Wrong $x$ coefficient ($+2x$ instead of $-8x$) and wrong constant. ✗
  • Choice D — $-4x^{9}-2x+5$: Wrong leading degree and wrong coefficients throughout. ✗
That confirms B is correct.
